events.title: Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others
events.description: The IDF announced it killed a Hamas Nukhba terrorist who participated in the abduction of hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin during the Oct. 7 attack.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others

events.title: A 'ceasefire war' in Lebanon continues as 'chaotic global leadership' takes its toll
events.description: Hezbollah's chief on Thursday rejected a conditional truce announced by Lebanese and Israeli envoys, demanding instead a comprehensive ceasefire and full Israeli withdrawal as he threatened northern Israel with new attacks. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A 'ceasefire war' in Lebanon continues as 'chaotic global leadership' takes its toll

events.title: Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support
events.description: Nearly half of the population in Government-controlled areas of Yemen are facing high levels of acute food insecurity with the crisis set to deepen further if international aid cuts continue, according to the latest analysis by the leading UN-backed global food security platform.

events.title: UN mission reports rising tensions along the Blue Line in Lebanon
events.description: Monday’s hastily convened meeting of the UN Security Council at the request of France in response to escalating violence in Lebanon between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants has underlined deepening international concern as the conflict intensifies, despite ongoing US mediation efforts.

events.title: US military kills two alleged narco-terrorists in latest Eastern Pacific strike on drug-trafficking vessel
events.description: U.S. military killed two alleged narco-terrorists in a lethal strike on a suspected drug-trafficking vessel in the Eastern Pacific, SOUTHCOM says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills two alleged narco-terrorists in latest Eastern Pacific strike on drug-trafficking vessel

events.title: Outrage in Argentina after two teen girls murdered as femicide crisis endures
events.description: Country is shaken by the brutal murders of two girls, aged 14 and 17, whose bodies were discovered just days apart Argentina has reacted with fury after the bodies of two murdered teenage girls were found just two days apart. The latest killings underscore the South American country’s enduring femicide crisis despite years of feminist campaigning, and have prompted alarm over the decision to cut support for victims of gender-based violence under the far-right administration of Javier Milei. Police found the remains of Agostina Vega, 14, on Saturday, in a field on the outskirts of the city of Córdoba. She had been fatally strangled and her body had been dismembered, according to local media reports. Continue reading...

events.title: Killed for speaking up: UN sounds alarm over attacks on environmental defenders
events.description: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk called on Thursday for greater protection for environmental and land defenders, noting that hundreds worldwide have been killed or detained in recent years.

events.title: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
events.description: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

events.title: Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO
events.description: The UN health agency in Lebanon is verifying reports of strikes on a hospital in the southern city of Tyre on Monday, amid a concerning rise in attacks on healthcare in the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO

events.title: Sherpa missing for a week on Everest found crawling toward base camp after his family begins funeral rites
events.description: Rescuers call it a miracle after a Sherpa guide survived nearly a week alone on Mount Everest without food, water or supplemental oxygen in the death zone.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sherpa missing for a week on Everest found crawling toward base camp after his family begins funeral rites

events.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response
events.description: Islamic State-linked militia blamed for raids in North Kivu as governor says three patients with disease fled clinics Rebel attacks around a town that is one of the centres of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have left more than 30 people dead over the past few days, complicating the response to the disease. At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

events.title: Hezbollah rejects latest ceasefire agreement as Israeli strikes kill 4 in Lebanon
events.description: Hezbollah on Thursday rejected the latest ceasefire agreement between Israel and the Lebanese government, and the militant group demanded a complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as more fighting there hampered efforts to end the Iran war. The Hezbollah announcement came as Israeli strikes killed at least four people, according to local authorities, and a UN peacekeeper was killed in the crossfire. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis reports from Beirut, Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah rejects latest ceasefire agreement as Israeli strikes kill 4 in Lebanon

events.title: ‘Women and girls cannot wait’: Lebanon’s crisis is putting lives at risk
events.description: Women and girls in Lebanon are paying an increasingly devastating price as violence and displacement continue despite a ceasefire, the UN reproductive health agency (UNFPA) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Women and girls cannot wait’: Lebanon’s crisis is putting lives at risk

events.title: Russia's economic forum opens against backdrop of Ukrainian strikes
events.description: The St. Petersburg International Economic Forum -- Russia's answer to Davos -- opens under the shadow of Ukrainian drone attacks and growing economic uncertainty.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia's economic forum opens against backdrop of Ukrainian strikes

events.title: Nauru issues rare statement after whistleblower alleges violent threats against Australia’s non-citizens
events.description: Nauran government says its citizens are ‘friendly’ after MP spoke of serious threats allegedly made against former Australian detainees Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Nauruan government has issued a rare statement insisting it is a “friendly” and “welcoming” country after a whistleblower alleged “serious threats of physical violence” were made against a group of non-citizens removed there by the Albanese government. The unexpected defence, sent shortly after midday Thursday, was mounted hours after independent MP Andrew Wilkie used his three-minute constituency statement to read claims from an anonymous whistleblower familiar with the arrangements of the secretive $2.5bn deal between Australia and Nauru. Continue reading...

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.title: Unsafe food kills 1.5 million people each year; children most at risk: WHO
events.description: Unsafe food causes an estimated 866 million illnesses and 1.5 million deaths every year worldwide, highlighting the often-overlooked toll of contaminated food on health, development and fragile economies, according to new data from the UN health agency.

events.title: Heroic ICE officers, staff member open up on growing dangers: ‘Am I going to die?'
events.description: ICE officers honored for heroism during the 2025 Dallas sniper attack speak out about growing threats and violence they say they now face nationwide.

events.title: World News in Brief: Violence hampers Ebola efforts, aid delivery in Darfur and Gaza, Nicaraguan activist’s death in custody
events.description: Escalating violence in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to impact civilians and efforts to fight Ebola, UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

events.title: Missing Sherpa guide found on Mount Everest after 'miracle' self-rescue
events.description: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

events.title: Russia kills 12 in Ukraine as Kyiv mourns 707 children killed since 2022

events.title: Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu
events.description: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expired Fierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee. In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds the city. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu

events.title: British 'mafia boss' kidnapped by law enforcement, lawyer claims

events.title: At least 21 killed in New Delhi hotel fire
events.description: At least 21 people have been killed after a fire tore through a hotel in the Indian capital New Delhi.

events.title: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
events.description: As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.

events.title: California high school graduation shooting kills teen, injures child and two others

events.title: As Hezbollah rejects truce, families on Israel's northern border describe life under fire

events.title: Bondi attack hero Ahmed al-Ahmed charged with assaulting his father
events.description: Police allege the 44-year-old lauded for disarming one of the gunmen in December’s attack put his father in a headlock Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Sydney man who disarmed one of the Bondi terror attackers has been charged with assaulting his father. Ahmed al-Ahmed, 44, is accused of allegedly putting his father in a headlock on 9 March at a house in Bankstown, in the city’s west. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bondi attack hero Ahmed al-Ahmed charged with assaulting his father

events.title: Mexico set for unprecedented third turn as World Cup host
events.description: Historically, the Mexican national team – known as ‘El Tri’ – has never exactly been renowned for its sporting achievements. The country reached the quarter-finals in 1970 and 1986. However, football is part of everyday life in every family and every village in Mexico. On 11 June, the country will host its third World Cup, a first in history, much to the delight of an entire nation that lives and breathes football.

events.title: African migrants flee as South Africa's xenophobic violence surges
events.description: Hundreds of foreigners fearing for their lives have taken shelter in community halls on South Africa's south coast, saying mobs of locals were going door-to-door telling them to leave the country. Mostly nationals of Malawi and Mozambique, many told AFP they had fled their homes at the weekend and spent nights in the mountains and bush, before making their way to the small-town community centres.

events.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it's unable to implement its monitoring responsibilities in Iran
events.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog has been unable to inspect nuclear facilities in Iran affected by the war last June, according to a confidential report by the U.N. nuclear watchdog
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN nuclear watchdog says it's unable to implement its monitoring responsibilities in Iran

events.title: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity
events.description: The UN human rights office, OHCHR, on Tuesday welcomed the opening of two specialised judicial units in Haiti, saying the move marked “a significant step toward tackling widespread impunity in the country.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity

events.title: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine
events.description: Overnight attacks in three key cities in Ukraine have left several civilians dead, scores more injured, and homes, hospitals and shops destroyed or damaged, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.

events.title: A fire at a nursing home in Sri Lanka kills 12 people

events.ticker_label: 🚑 A fire at a nursing home in Sri Lanka kills 12 people

events.title: Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim
events.description: North Carolina judge said Levi Mendez-Maldonado failed to show up in court – even after being told he had died in 2024 An immigration judge in Charlotte, North Carolina , recently ordered the deportation of a young man who was killed in 2024, citing his failure to appear in court. Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting in November 2024. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim

events.title: World News in Brief: UN urges restraint as Gulf tensions rise, fear and uncertainty in Lebanon, hunger grows in the Sahel
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres expressed alarm on Tuesday over reported overnight exchanges of fire between the United States and Iran, as well as reports that Iran targeted Kuwait and Bahrain.

events.title: 2026 World Cup: Senegal fans fear United States visa barrier
events.description: Across Africa, many believe it will be difficult for fans to get visas to the World Cup. The fear is growing in Senegal – who were put on the United States immigration blacklist in late 2025 by Donald Trump. Senegalese nationals are no longer eligible for a US visa except in a few limited cases and visa applications have been systematically rejected since then. Even 6 members of the executive committee of the Senegalese Football Federation were denied entry to the United States.
events.ticker_label: 💥 2026 World Cup: Senegal fans fear United States visa barrier

events.title: World Ocean Assessment highlights urgent need for science and global action
events.description: In the wood-panelled rooms of New York’s storied Explorers Club, where portraits of pioneering adventurers line the walls, diplomats, scientists and ocean advocates gathered on Tuesday evening with a shared sense of urgency – and anticipation.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World Ocean Assessment highlights urgent need for science and global action

events.title: Lebanon: Another peacekeeper dies in new attack

events.ticker_label: 💥 Lebanon: Another peacekeeper dies in new attack

events.title: UN General Assembly elects Bangladesh’s Rahman as next president
events.description: Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman was elected President of the UN General Assembly’s 81st session on Tuesday after defeating Andreas Kakouris of Cyprus in a closely contested vote, positioning himself to steer the world body through a pivotal year marked by intensifying global crises, UN reform efforts and major leadership transitions.

events.title: Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest
events.description: Andrew Wilson appears in court over killing of Latoya Bulgin at protest over a police shooting days earlier Authorities in Jamaica have taken the rare step of charging a police officer with murder after he was accused of shooting a 45-year-old woman in a case that prompted violent protests. According to the Independent Commission of Investigations (Indecom), Constable Andrew Wilson appeared in court on Wednesday and was denied bail. Another hearing is scheduled for mid-June. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest

events.title: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
events.description: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’ Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance. Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off – with US encouragement – its pacifist outlook in place since the end of the second world war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China

events.title: A 'rogues' gallery' of notorious figures at Russia's biggest economic conference
events.description: Russia-U.S. talks on economic and energy issues continue despite a pause in Ukraine peace negotiations, Putin's special envoy Kirill Dmitriev told ​Reuters on Thursday, ‌adding he spoke to U.S. counterparts a day earlier. He spoke on the sidelines of the St Petersburg economic summit, where, as FRANCE 24's international affairs commentator Douglas Herbert explains, a veritable 'rogues' gallery' of notorious figures showed up in support of Putin's policies.

events.title: War in Sudan: Is the unity of the RSF beginning to crack?
events.description: In recent days, the Sudanese army has announced advances in the east of the country, recapturing several towns from the Rapid Support Forces and forcing some of their members to cross the border into Ethiopia to seek refuge there. At the same time, numerous sources in Darfur report tensions within the paramilitary group, which are said to have led to the defection of several commanders over the past few weeks. Is the unity of the RSF beginning to crack? FRANCE 24’s Bastien Renouil reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in Sudan: Is the unity of the RSF beginning to crack?

events.title: North Korea unveils another nuclear plant
events.description: North Korea says leader Kim Jong Un has visited a new facility to produce nuclear materials and announced plans to bolster the country’s nuclear forces “at an exponential rate."

events.title: Gaza’s public servants systematically targeted in Israeli strikes
events.description: Months since Gaza’s nominal ceasefire began, Palestinians continue to be killed and maimed in drone and airstrikes, including the enclave’s police force which is crucial to peace and reconstruction efforts, the UN human rights office (OHCHR) said on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza’s public servants systematically targeted in Israeli strikes

events.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching up The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue reading...

events.title: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
events.description: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plastics Takeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis. The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and price rises and shortages are hitting hard across the industry and beyond. Some outlets have begun offering perks to customers who bring their own bags, plates or containers. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens

events.title: Iran war: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel ceasefire terms
events.description: The Iran-backed group Hezbollah said it will reject any deal allowing Israel to stay in southern Lebanon, while Israel said it won't withdraw from the area near the border. Follow DW for more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel ceasefire terms

events.title: Tiananmen dissident lambasts China on massacre anniversary
events.description: On the anniversary of the Tiananmen Square massacre, Wu'er Kaixi, who played a leading role during the 1989 protests, spoke to DW about his personal loss and nearly four decades in exile.

events.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
events.description: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch

events.title: Massive disinformation campaign underway in Armenia ahead of elections, analysts warn
events.description: One ballot box, two competing battles. In Armenia, voters are heading to the polls to decide the future direction of their country. But beneath the political campaigns lies a far more insidious warfare. Analysts warn that a massive, coordinated network of foreign disinformation - much of it tracing back to Russia - is systematically weaponizing the deep-seated trauma of recent wars. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports from Yerevan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Massive disinformation campaign underway in Armenia ahead of elections, analysts warn

events.title: Sherpa guide missing for a week on Mount Everest rescued while crawling to base camp
events.description: Rescuers say a Sherpa guide was found alive a week after he went missing on Mount Everest

events.title: Growing up with sirens: UN child rights envoy on the toll of the Ukraine-Russia war
events.description: Children in Ukraine have been profoundly impacted by years of war, sheltering in underground schools – or forced to study online – and living with the psychological strain of constant air raid sirens that could spell death for them and their families.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Growing up with sirens: UN child rights envoy on the toll of the Ukraine-Russia war

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside

events.title: DR Congo opposition calls for protest against Tshisekedi running for 3rd term

events.title: ‘We are catching up’ – WHO chief on DR Congo’s Ebola fight
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Wednesday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is showing signs of progress – but significant challenges remain in testing, surveillance, vaccine development and building community trust.

events.title: Karmelo Anthony trial ignites dueling crowds turning courthouse into shouting match in racially-charged case

events.title: ‘Unpatriotic’: Trump decries Republicans who voted to constrain Iran war

events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Unpatriotic’: Trump decries Republicans who voted to constrain Iran war

events.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
events.description: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.” Continue reading...

events.title: Kuwait says Iranian drones hit airport and killed 1 as ceasefire is tested again
events.description: Kuwait says Iranian drones have heavily damaged a passenger terminal at its main airport, killing one person, wounding dozens and briefly closing the airfield. Iran denies damaging the airport.

events.title: India news: Delhi orders fire safety crackdown after deadly hotel fire

events.title: Trump slams 'unpatriotic' US House vote to end Iran war
events.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday slammed a vote in the US House seeking to order the withdrawal of American troops from the Iran war, suggesting the "unpatriotic" move disrupted negotiations with Tehran. The largely symbolic vote came "right in the middle of my final negotiations to end the War with the Islamic Republic of Iran," Trump said in a post on his Truth Social platform.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump slams 'unpatriotic' US House vote to end Iran war

events.title: El Niño confirmed, set to fuel more extreme weather, says WMO
events.description: The UN urged all countries on Tuesday to bolster early warning systems after confirming the onset of El Niño, warning that the Pacific Ocean-warming phenomenon will bring above-average temperatures “nearly everywhere” and fuel more extreme weather.

events.title: Cancer breakthrough bonanza: Does string of advances signal turning point?
events.description: It’s not every day that a standing ovation at a medical conference goes viral. Dateline Chicago and the annual conference of the American Society of Clinical Oncology. Doctors from around the world out of their seats to cheer a new pill that doubles the survival time of pancreatic cancer, one of the deadliest forms of the disease. At the same conference, big announcements on breast cancer bladder cancer, neck cancer, and more. Why all the breakthroughs?

events.title: Artificial Intelligence: Open-source important to 'Europe's tech strength'

events.title: Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya

Soon after the US revealed it was setting up a field hospital in Kenya for the Ebola quarantine and treatment of Americans, the Kenyan high court blocked the order – but the Kenyan and US governments moved forward anyway, with the first American responders reportedly landing at the Laikipia airbase on Saturday. events.title: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales

The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US , ending whale captivity in Canada. events.description: Austria and Portugal have won seats on the UN Security Council, ending Germany's 40-year streak. DW examines the foreign policy questions raised by Berlin's defeat.

events.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
events.description: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

events.title: Germany news: Number of millionaires is growing, study shows
events.description: Around 1.78 million people in Germany were classified as high-net-worth individuals or people who have at least $1 million in liquid assets. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany news: Number of millionaires is growing, study shows

events.title: $850 million Obama Presidential Center prepares for opening
events.description: The museum dedicated to former US President Barack Obama is set to open on June 19. Spanning 19.3 acres, the campus will house a museum, library, and park.

events.title: China bans 4 New Zealand lawmakers after their Taiwan trip
events.description: Beijing says it is barring a group of New Zealand lawmakers from entering China for having visited Taiwan in May. It is the first time such a ban has been imposed on politicians from New Zealand.

events.title: Ukraine's Zelensky pledges clear timetable of talks on EU membership
events.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky ⁠pledged on Thursday that ​Ukraine would keep to a clear timetable in talks on joining the ​European Union and in sticking to its obligations with the 27-nation bloc. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Georgia's former minister of Euro-Atlantic integration and professor at SciencesPo Paris Tornike Gorgadze.

events.title: Greenland is Denmark's 'for now', says America's top diplomat
events.description: "For now."

events.title: Judge blocks Jewish group's attempt to stop Kanye West gigs
events.description: Kanye West will be allowed to perform two gigs in the Netherlands after a judge shot down a Jewish group's attempt to block them.

events.title: A Yazidi tells an Australian court she was enslaved and raped in an IS home in Syria
events.description: A Yazidi woman alleges she was enslaved in Syria by a woman and repeatedly raped and beaten by the woman’s father, according to police

events.title: Gunmen kidnap 7 students from school in northwestern Nigeria
events.description: Gunmen have raided an off-campus residence in northwest Nigeria and kidnapped seven students

events.title: China bans 4 New Zealand lawmakers after they visited Taiwan
events.description: Beijing has banned four New Zealand lawmakers from traveling to China for a year, demanding an apology for their visit to Taiwan

events.title: How Voodoo overcame suppression and became a democratic force in the West African nation of Benin
events.description: Benin recently inaugurated President Romuald Wadagni as its new leader in the latest transition in the West African nation

events.title: Kyrgyzstan wins seat on UN Security Council for first time ever
events.description: A closely watched Security Council election delivered a mix of continuity and change on Wednesday, as Austria, Portugal, Trinidad and Tobago, Zimbabwe – and first-time member Kyrgyzstan, secured seats around the iconic horseshoe table.

events.title: FBI launches 'Most Wanted Fraudsters' list as DOJ charges Ohio defendants in $30M Medicaid scheme for kids
events.description: DOJ unveils major fraud crackdown including a $30 million Ohio Medicaid scheme allegedly funding luxury cars and the FBI's new Most Wanted Fraudsters list.

events.title: Man found with zip ties and duct tape charged with assaulting five women on UCLA campus in under an hour
events.description: A man allegedly sexually assaulted multiple women on the UCLA campus in under an hour and was found with zip ties and duct tape, prosecutors say.

events.title: President Trump announces pick for next attorney general and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Frontier passengers tackle man who allegedly choked off-duty flight attendant, tried to open door mid-flight
events.description: Juan Gabriel Reyes, 51, allegedly tried to open exit doors mid-flight and choked a flight attendant on a Frontier Airlines plane before being restrained.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Frontier passengers tackle man who allegedly choked off-duty flight attendant, tried to open door mid-flight

events.title: Trump expands Cuba sanctions beyond US companies in major crackdown on foreign enablers
events.description: Trump administration rolls out unprecedented secondary sanctions on Cuba targeting foreign firms doing business with military-linked conglomerate GAESA.

events.title: Bodycam footage fuels backlash after police handcuff dying stabbing victim
events.description: Bodycam footage shows English police handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak after he said he was stabbed and could not breathe moments before dying.

events.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
events.description: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

events.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
events.description: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

events.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
events.description: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.

events.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
events.description: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.
